L1.area: Error handling and process lifecycle management

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the process termination logic when running inside a Docker container. The system call to retrieve the process group ID fails in containerized environments, resulting in a nil pointer that causes a segmentation fault (SIGSEGV) and denial of service.

L3.area: Error handling and process termination routines for signal-based process exit
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the process termination logic when running inside a Docker container. The system call to retrieve the process group ID for sending SIGTERM to the process group fails in containerized environments because the process namespace differs from standard host execution, resulting in a nil pointer that causes a segmentation fault (SIGSEGV) and denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The Abort function calls syscall.Getpgid which can return a nil pointer in Docker containers where process group IDs cannot be properly resolved, causing a SIGSEGV denial of service.
